notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateOceInstance, GetOceInstance, ListOceInstances, ChangeOceInstanceCompartment, UpdateOceInstance, DeleteOceInstance, and GetWorkRequest are present in v65.110.0. The mutating responses expose opc-work-request-id but do not return an OceInstance body, and ListOceInstances filters compartmentId, tenancyId, displayName, and lifecycleState while returning Items []OceInstanceSummary that project the resource name as name.

Pinned provider surface: the Terraform resource is `oci_oce_oce_instance`, with matching singular and list data sources `oci_oce_oce_instance` and `oci_oce_oce_instances` for the same one-resource service family.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create stays explicit: when no OCI identifier is tracked, the runtime uses paginated ListOceInstances matches on exact compartmentId plus tenancyId plus displayName=spec.name, ignores FAILED, DELETING, and DELETED summaries, and fails on duplicate exact-name matches instead of adopting arbitrarily.

Repo-authored mutation policy stays explicit: only addOnFeatures, description, drRegion, freeformTags, definedTags, instanceLicenseType, instanceUsageType, and wafPrimaryDomain reconcile in place. `ChangeOceInstanceCompartment` remains out-of-scope drift for the published runtime, `IdcsAccessToken` remains create-time-only because OCI does not echo it back after create, and `LifecycleDetails` stays status-projected service state rather than a supported user-driven update input.

Repo-authored async handling stays explicit: create, update, and delete all use service-sdk GetWorkRequest polling plus work-request resource OCID recovery before the runtime rereads GetOceInstance, while delete confirmation still requires GetOceInstance or fallback ListOceInstances to observe DELETED or NotFound.
